summary:
The patient’s symptoms—chronic stress, inability to relax, racing thoughts, impaired focus, and impulsive shopping for brief relief—are most consistent with generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), particularly given their link to work-related stressor exposure. The episode of shoplifting reflects maladaptive emotional regulation under distress, not fulfilling diagnostic requirements for kleptomania or OCD, making cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) the optimal first-line treatment approach.  

\boxed{D}
